metrics.narrative: Bandhan Bank's core pain is a fully manual, email-based RFI/RFP and vendor-negotiation process covering 400+ active vendors and 2,000+ purchase orders a month with a lean 4-person negotiation team (Sayantan Mukherjee, Introductory Call, 1 Feb 2025). The driver is an RBI regulatory mandate for formal vendor performance/SLA management and reporting, not yet quantified in the buyer's own numbers. Zycus proposed an 8-10% cycle-time/PR-to-PO savings estimate (Solution Overview call, 5 Feb 2025), but this is Zycus's own claim, not yet buyer-validated, and the CFO is expected to demand a costed ROI case at the 13 Jul 2026 presentation (Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026: 'for a CFO that's a big deal to find out what is the cost of implementations'). Quantifying savings/ROI specifically for the CFO ahead of 13 Jul is the critical open action.

champion.narrative: Nishan Kumar (DVP, Strategy & Program Management) is the clear process champion: he has run point on every stage since Apr 2026, proactively briefed Zycus on committee feedback and CFO-meeting prep, and gave Zycus 'a second chance' to address the implementation gap rather than letting the panel's score eliminate it (2 Jul 2026: 'this was the only red flag... we decided you'll get another chance'). However, he explicitly told Zycus this coaching is not exclusive ('I'm saying this to every other vendor as well,' 2 Jul 2026), so his advocacy is best read as developing rather than proven partisan support: he is a strong, reliable information conduit but has not been observed pushing the CFO toward Zycus specifically over the other two finalists.

competition.narrative: Zycus is one of 3 remaining finalists (down from roughly 20) as of 8 Jul 2026 and was rated #1 on product and technology by the 5-member Technical Committee (Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026); the sole scored weakness is implementation, specifically the absence of India BFSI (banking) delivery experience, with at least one rival finalist reportedly bringing a named consultant with peer-bank (Axis/IDFC-type) project credentials. Named alternatives across the evaluation include SAP Ariba, iValua and Coupa (Sayantan Mukherjee, Discovery Call, 7 Feb 2025) and GEP Worldwide (Competitors__c); Oracle Fusion was also referenced internally as a market alternative to prepare a comparison against (P2P Demo prep notes, 17 Jun 2026). The wedge is Zycus's AI/agentic capability and Gartner #1 ranking, already validated as the standout demo of all vendors evaluated ('you were amazing that day... it was probably the best presentation of all 7 vendors,' Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026); the risk is losing on the last scored dimension despite winning on product and technology.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed, buyer-articulated pain: procurement is run manually by email for RFI/RFP with no centralized negotiation process or audit trail (Sayantan Mukherjee, Discovery Call, 7 Feb 2025: 'RFI, RFPs were still now are being done on email... somewhere down the track we lose track of... communication'). Manual invoice/vendor-onboarding workflows and a lack of real-time visibility/reporting were reconfirmed as primary pain points in the 17 Jun 2026 demo. The pain owner is Sayantan Mukherjee (Contact.Title: Branch Credit Manager; described across calls as leading the bank's IT procurement/strategy function), reinforced by an RBI regulatory mandate driving urgency.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA/SOW/legal activity has started; the deal is still in vendor selection with commercials explicitly deferred until after the 13 Jul 2026 CFO presentation (Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026: 'RFQ thing will happen post our call with CFO and panel'). Budget is stated as open/uncapped ('we have kept an open budget,' Nishan Kumar, 10 Apr 2026), which removes a paper-process budget gate, but no procurement/legal/InfoSec workflow has engaged yet. The only contracting-adjacent detail confirmed is the India data-residency requirement (NTT Global, Mumbai data center) discussed in the 16 Mar 2026 proposal-queries call. Visibility on SI/partner requirements and legal review would only emerge post-13-Jul.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the CFO (Rajeev Mantri, per Salesforce contact and repeated Avoma references), who chairs a 5-member Technical Committee alongside the CIO, per Nishan Kumar's 10 Apr 2026 briefing. As of today (8 Jul 2026) the CFO and the CIO have had zero direct engagement with Zycus; the first face-to-face exposure is the 13 Jul 2026 Mumbai session, a 45-minute business-credentials presentation with a hard one-hour stop (Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026 call). All EB signal to date is second-hand, relayed through Nishan Kumar rather than direct EB contact, so EB engagement is confirmed as a process gate but not yet as a relationship.

decision_process.narrative: The process is transparent and buyer-mapped: roughly 20 initial vendors were narrowed to 6 (10 Apr 2026) via technical-committee scoring, then to a two-hour deep-dive demo (17 Jun 2026) attended by Bandhan's full evaluation team, then narrowed further to the current final field of 3 (Next_Step__c, 8 Jul 2026), with the decisive step now a 45-minute CFO/CIO business-credentials session on 13 Jul 2026 in Mumbai (hard one-hour stop, Nishan Kumar, 2 Jul 2026). Rising seniority on both sides (Zycus's regional leadership joining every call; the CFO/CIO now the gate on the buyer side) signals the process has moved from functional evaluation to executive sign-off. Commercials/RFQ are explicitly deferred until after the CFO session.

sources: Avoma Next Stage Update call 10 Apr 2026
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and buyer-stated: a five-member Technical Committee scores vendors on (1) effectiveness at solving current process problems, (2) cost/efficiency gains in days and headcount, (3) depth of AI/automation (RPA), and (4) future roadmap innovation (Nishan Kumar, Next Stage Update call, 10 Apr 2026). At the 2 Jul 2026 checkpoint, Nishan confirmed Zycus scored #1 on both product and technology of the three tracked criteria (product, implementation, technology), with implementation the only sub-criterion scoring lower due to the missing India banking delivery track record.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 15+ months of dated rep notes covering RFP receipt, response, shortlisting and management presentation, there is zero buyer-side mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, autonomous, or any AI capability. The 17 Feb 2026 rep note records: 'Spoke to Mrinmoy Mahapatra from Bandhan Bank... told that we have filled the responses to the RFP shared by them... and clarified them to ask if they have any further queries about it.' The dialogue is entirely RFP-procedural, not AI-themed.
• On 9 Apr 2026 nishan.konar @bandhanbank.com confirmed: 'we have been shortlisted to top 6 vendors next round.' On 28 Apr 2026: 'We got the invitation from Dilip (Bandhan Bank) for presenting to their management around 10th May. Meeting will focus on Zycus differentiation and platform Demo.' Even the management pitch is framed as 'Zycus differentiation and platform Demo' ‚Äî no AI use cases, no Merlin demo focus, no agentic conversation despite Merlin being in product scope.
• 9 Avoma meetings with verified @bandhanbank.com attendees corroborate the engagement is real but generic. Confidence is High because the NextStep is unusually rich (4,381 chars across 15+ touchpoints) and the absence of any AI keyword across that volume is itself strong evidence. Merlin appears in Products but never in conversation; this is a traditional BFSI P2P RFP, not an AI-driven evaluation.
